NCT02142283.name: DAWN
NCT02142283.group: AIS 6-24 h from LKW with LVO + clinical-core mismatch, thrombectomy + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)
NCT02142283.snippet: Source: Nogueira RG et al. NEJM 2018;378:11-21 (DAWN).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 206 adults with acute ischaemic stroke last-known-well 6-24 hours prior, with proximal intracranial ICA or M1 occlusion AND clinical-core mismatch (NIHSS >=10 + small infarct on CT-perfusion / MR-DWI) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + medical management (n=107) or medical management alone (n=99). Trial terminated early for efficacy at pre-specified interim.

evidence.label: Primary - Utility-Weighted mRS and mRS 0-2
evidence.text: Co-primary utility-weighted modified Rankin Scale at 90 days: thrombectomy 5.5 vs control 3.4 (Bayesian posterior probability of superiority >0.999). Co-primary functional independence (mRS 0-2 at 90 days): thrombectomy 49% (52/107) vs control 13% (13/99); risk ratio 3.71 (95% CI 2.16-6.38). Both co-primaries met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Safety (sICH, 90-day Mortality)
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 6% vs control 3% (NS). 90-day mortality: 19% vs 18% (NS). No increase in serious adverse events. Number needed to treat for mRS 0-2 ~2.8.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by age and core volume. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label allocation (unavoidable for surgical intervention), mitigated by blinded 90-day mRS adjudicator + independent central review. D3 LOW - 100% mRS ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded core reviewer. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "General Inclusion Criteria:~1. Clinical signs and symptoms consistent with the diagnosis of an acute ischemic stroke, and subject belongs to one of the following subgroups:~   1. Subject has failed IV t-PA therapy (defined as a confirmed persistent occlusion 60 min after administration)~  2. Subject is contraindicated for IV t-PA administration~2. Age ≥18~3. Baseline NIHSS ≥10 (assessed within one hour of measuring core infarct volume)~4. Subject can be randomized between with 6 to 24 hours aft...

NCT02586415.name: DEFUSE 3
NCT02586415.group: AIS 6-16 h from LKW with LVO + perfusion-imaging mismatch, thrombectomy + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)
NCT02586415.snippet: Source: Albers GW et al. NEJM 2018;378:708-718 (DEFUSE 3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 207 adults with acute ischaemic stroke 6-16 hours from last-known-well with proximal anterior-circulation LVO AND perfusion-imaging target mismatch (core <70 mL, mismatch ratio >=1.8, mismatch volume >=15 mL on RAPID CT-perfusion or MR-DWI-PWI) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + standard medical (n=115) or standard medical alone (n=92). Trial terminated early at pre-specified interim.

evidence.label: Primary - mRS Shift and mRS 0-2
evidence.text: Co-primary ordinal mRS at 90 days favoured thrombectomy (common odds ratio 2.77, 95% CI 1.63-4.70; P<0.001). Functional independence (mRS 0-2 at 90 days): thrombectomy 45% (52/115) vs control 17% (16/92); risk ratio 2.60 (95% CI 1.59-4.26). Both co-primaries met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH, Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 7% vs control 4% (NS). 90-day mortality: 14% vs 26% (P=0.05, favouring thrombectomy). No excess serious AE. NNT for mRS 0-2 ~3.6.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical intervention), mitigated by blinded 90-day mRS adjudication. D3 LOW - 99% mRS ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded adjudicators. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 90 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Clinical Inclusion Criteria:~1. Signs & symptoms consistent w/ the diagnosis of acute anterior circulation ischemic stroke~2. Age 18-90 years~3. Baseline NIHSSS is ≥ 6 and remains ≥6 immediately prior to randomization~4. Endovascular treatment can be initiated (femoral puncture) between 6 and 16 hours of stroke onset. Stroke onset is defined as the time the patient was last known to be at their neurologic baseline (wake-up strokes are eligible if they meet the above time limits).~5. modified R...

NULLED:NCT03098355.name: MR CLEAN-LATE
NULLED:NCT03098355.group: AIS 6-24 h from LKW with LVO + collateral-based selection, thrombectomy + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-2 RR)
NULLED:NCT03098355.snippet: Source: Olthuis SGH et al. Lancet 2023;401:1371-1380 (MR CLEAN-LATE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 502 adults with acute ischaemic stroke 6-24 hours from last-known-well with anterior-circulation LVO and presence of collateral flow on CT-angiography (any collateral score >0) randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + medical management (n=255) or medical management alone (n=247). Collateral-based selection; no perfusion-imaging mismatch required.

evidence.label: Primary - mRS Shift at 90 Days
evidence.text: Primary ordinal mRS shift at 90 days: adjusted common odds ratio 1.67 (95% CI 1.20-2.32; P=0.0024), favouring thrombectomy. Functional independence (mRS 0-2): thrombectomy 39% (98/255) vs control 27% (66/247); adjusted risk ratio 1.43 (95% CI 1.10-1.87). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 6.7% vs control 1.6% (P=0.006). 90-day mortality: 24% vs 30% (NS). Effect smaller than DAWN/DEFUSE 3 reflecting broader less-strict imaging selection.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical), mitigated by blinded 90-day mRS. D3 LOW - 99% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 1 Year; max age 14 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Expected survival>12 weeks.~7. Histologically confirmed as CD19/20-positive ALL/NHL and who meet one of the following conditions:~   1. title: RapidMeta Neurology | Endovascular Thrombectomy in Extended (6-24 h) Window Stroke v1.1
